channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: A weekly roundup of political cartoons capturing key events in U.S. politics
item.description: The article presents a curated gallery of editorial cartoons from political cartoonists across the United States, each offering a satirical take on the most prominent political developments of the wee...

item.title: Keeley Hawes and Paapa Essiedu discuss the human side of priests and nuns in Jack Thorne’s Falling
item.description: Jack Thorne’s new romantic drama Falling explores the complex lives of a nun, Anna, played by Keeley Hawes, and a priest, David, portrayed by Paapa Essiedu. Set in a UK convent, the series delves into...

item.title: Truecaller reduces workforce by 15% following revenue drop in Q1 2026
item.description: Sweden-based caller ID and communication platform Truecaller announced it will lay off 70 employees, representing approximately 15% of its workforce, during the second quarter of 2026. This decision f...

item.title: Ex-Aide to Bello Matawalle Accuses Police Officers of Rights Abuse, Extortion and Threats Over Dadiyata Disappearance
item.description: Musa Muhammad Kamarawa, wey be former Senior Special Assistant to ex-Zamfara Governor and current Minister of State for Defence, Bello Matawalle, don come out with serious allegations against two seni...

item.title: House Committee to Sponsor VP Sara Duterte Impeachment Report on May 11
item.description: The House Committee on Justice, led by Chairperson Representative Gerville Luistro, will sponsor the committee report and resolution on the Articles of Impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte ...

item.title: Tom Dearden suffers serious injury after being tackled from behind by two Eels players
item.description: During a recent NRL match, Brisbane Broncos' young playmaker Tom Dearden was left in visible pain after a dangerous tackle from behind by two Parramatta Eels players. The incident occurred when both E...

item.title: Man Arrested for 1986 Murder of Teenager Following DNA Breakthrough
item.description: Authorities in Montgomery County, Texas, have arrested 60-year-old Bobby Charles Taylor Sr., who has been charged with capital murder in connection with the 1986 rape and killing of 16-year-old Deanna...

item.title: Pakistan Security Forces Neutralise Five Terrorists in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Operations
item.description: On 7th and 8th May 2026, Pakistan security forces successfully carried out two intelligence-based operations (IBOs) in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, targeting terrorists reportedly sponsored by India. The first...

item.title: Seventeen hikers rescued after Mount Dukono eruption in Indonesia, search continues for three missing
item.description: Indonesian authorities are continuing their search for three missing hikers following a significant volcanic eruption at Mount Dukono on Halmahera Island in North Maluku province on Friday morning. Th...

item.title: UPI founder Poquiz defends alleged ex-Marines amid controversy over cash deliveries
item.description: Retired Major General Romeo Poquiz, founding chairperson of the United People's Initiative (UPI), spoke out on Friday regarding alleged large-scale cash deliveries to government officials, calling att...

item.title: Community ATV Ride Marks Opening of Tay Township Trail
item.description: The town of Tay Township is marking the opening of its newest recreational trail with a community ATV ride in Barrie. The event aims to bring together local ATV enthusiasts, families, and residents to...

item.title: Marina Hyde on Labour's poor election results and calls to replace Keir Starmer
item.description: In her latest column, Guardian writer Marina Hyde uses an aviation metaphor to criticise the current state of the Labour Party under Keir Starmer's leadership. Following disappointing May election res...

item.title: Federal High Court Orders Sowore to Open Defence in Cyberbullying Case
item.description: For Abuja, the Federal High Court on Friday reject the no-case submission wey Omoyele Sowore carry come. Sowore, wey be activist and publisher, dey face charges for cyberbullying President Bola Tinubu...

item.title: Guide to Sorghum Farming and Earnings Per Acre in Kenya
item.description: Sorghum is Kenya’s fifth most important grain crop after maize, wheat, rice and barley. It is grown both as a staple food and a cash crop, and also serves as fodder for livestock. The crop is drought-...

item.title: Khampepe Commission continues investigation into stalled TRC prosecutions
item.description: The Khampepe Commission of Inquiry resumed proceedings on Friday in Newtown, Johannesburg, focusing on uncovering attempts to obstruct investigations and prosecutions of individuals implicated in apar...

item.title: WHO expects limited hantavirus outbreak linked to MV Hondius cruise ship as more cases possible due to incubation period
item.description: The World Health Organization has stated that additional cases of hantavirus may emerge following an outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship that has resulted in three deaths. The virus involved is the...

item.title: Fact Check: Photos of Linet Chepkorir Being Body-Shamed Are Altered
item.description: Social media in Kenya has been circulating two images that allegedly show Bomet Woman Representative Linet Chepkorir, popularly known as Linet Toto, having gained significant weight. These images have...

item.title: Conservation officer uses creative methods to reduce human-wildlife conflict in Mikumi
item.description: In the villages surrounding Mikumi National Park, human-wildlife conflict has been a major issue, with crocodiles, elephants, lions, and hyenas frequently entering human areas. Makene Ngoroma, a local...

item.title: RappLearn Offers Executive Training in Journalism, AI, and Leadership
item.description: RappLearn, the learning and training arm of Rappler, provides world-class programs designed to equip professionals, leaders, and organizations with practical skills and insights rooted in real-world e...

item.title: Julie Bishop Steps Down as ANU Chancellor Amid Controversies
item.description: Julie Bishop has officially resigned from her role as chancellor of the Australian National University, concluding a period marked by controversies and criticism. During her tenure, Bishop faced scrut...
